site.name: VaultConvert
site.tagline: Free, private file converter that runs entirely in your browser. Your files never leave your device.

tools.title: HEIC to JPG
tools.metaDesc: Convert HEIC to JPG free, right in your browser. No upload, no watermark, no sign-up — your photos never leave your device.
tools.intro: HEIC is the format the iPhone uses to save photos, and it looks great until you try to open it on Windows, attach it to an email, or upload it somewhere that only accepts JPG. This converter turns HEIC (and HEIF) photos into universally compatible JPG files instantly. Everything happens locally in your browser using your device's own processing power — your photos are never uploaded to any server, which matters a lot when the files are personal pictures.
howto: Drag your HEIC photos into the box above, or click to choose them.
VaultConvert decodes each photo in your browser — nothing is uploaded.
Download your JPG files individually or all at once.

faq.q: Are my photos uploaded to a server?
faq.a: No. The conversion runs entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Your photos never leave your device, so there is nothing to leak or delete.

faq.q: Will I lose quality converting HEIC to JPG?
faq.a: JPG is a lossy format, but we encode at 92% quality, which keeps the result visually identical for almost all photos while staying compatible everywhere.

faq.q: Can I convert many HEIC files at once?
faq.a: Yes. Drop as many as you like and download them together. See the batch page for tips on large sets.

modifiers.label: on iPhone
modifiers.intro: Your iPhone saves photos as HEIC to save space, but that format trips up a lot of apps and websites. You don't need an app from the App Store to fix it — this page works directly in Safari on your iPhone. Open it, pick the photos from your camera roll, and get JPG files you can share anywhere. Because the conversion happens on the phone itself, your personal photos are never sent to a server.
howto: Open this page in Safari on your iPhone.
Tap the box and choose Photo Library, then select your HEIC photos.
Tap each converted JPG and use the Share icon to save it back to Photos or send it.

faq.q: Do I need to install an app?
faq.a: No. This runs in Safari directly. There is nothing to download from the App Store.

faq.q: Why does my iPhone save photos as HEIC?
faq.a: HEIC stores the same quality in roughly half the file size. You can also switch to JPG in Settings > Camera > Formats > Most Compatible.

modifiers.label: on Mac
modifiers.intro: On a Mac you can open HEIC in Preview, but exporting each photo by hand is slow, and you may not want to install a converter app just for this. This page does it in your browser — Safari, Chrome, or Firefox — with no app and no upload. Drop a folder of HEIC photos and get JPGs in seconds.
howto: Open this page in your Mac browser.
Drag HEIC files from Finder straight into the box.
Download the JPGs to your Downloads folder.

faq.q: Why not just use Preview?
faq.a: Preview works for one file at a time. This handles many at once and needs no manual Export step for each photo.

modifiers.label: on Windows
modifiers.intro: Windows can't open HEIC out of the box — it asks you to buy the HEVC codec from the Microsoft Store. Skip that entirely. This converter decodes HEIC in your browser on any Windows PC, so you get JPG files without paying for a codec or installing software.
howto: Open this page in Edge, Chrome, or Firefox on Windows.
Drag your HEIC files into the box.
Download the converted JPGs.

faq.q: Do I need the paid HEVC extension?
faq.a: No. Because we decode HEIC in the browser, you don't need any Windows codec or store purchase.

modifiers.label: in batch
modifiers.intro: Converting one photo at a time is fine, but most people have a whole camera roll of HEIC files. This page is tuned for batches: drop dozens of HEIC photos at once and download them as a set. Since processing is local, large batches are limited only by your device's memory, not by an upload limit.
howto: Select or drag many HEIC files at once.
Wait for each to finish — progress shows per file.
Use Download all to grab the whole set.

faq.q: Is there a file count limit?
faq.a: There is no hard server limit because nothing is uploaded. Very large batches depend on your device's available memory.

modifiers.label: without losing quality
modifiers.intro: JPG is a lossy format, so a careless converter can soften details or add artifacts. This page encodes at high quality (92%) and preserves the full resolution of your HEIC photo, so the JPG looks visually identical at normal viewing sizes. If you need a perfectly lossless result, convert to PNG instead.
howto: Drop your HEIC photo into the box.
We keep full resolution and encode at high JPG quality.
Download the JPG — compare it side by side with the original.

faq.q: Is JPG ever truly lossless?
faq.a: No, JPG always discards some data. For a lossless copy choose HEIC to PNG instead, at the cost of a larger file.

tools.title: HEIC to PNG
tools.metaDesc: Convert HEIC to PNG free in your browser. Lossless output, no upload, no watermark.
tools.intro: PNG is a lossless format, so converting HEIC to PNG keeps every pixel of your original photo intact — useful for editing, screenshots, or images with sharp edges and text. As with all VaultConvert tools, the conversion runs entirely in your browser and your photos are never uploaded.
howto: Drag your HEIC photos into the box above.
Each photo is decoded locally and re-encoded as lossless PNG.
Download your PNG files.

faq.q: Why choose PNG over JPG?
faq.a: PNG is lossless and supports transparency, so it is better for editing or graphics. JPG is smaller and better for sharing photos.

faq.q: Are PNG files larger?
faq.a: Usually yes — lossless compression means bigger files than JPG, especially for photographs.

modifiers.label: on iPhone
modifiers.intro: If you want to edit, annotate, or crop an iPhone photo without losing any quality, PNG is the format to use because it is lossless. iPhones capture in HEIC, and this page turns those photos into lossless PNGs right inside Safari on your phone — no App Store download and no upload. Your photos are processed on the device itself.
howto: Open this page in Safari on your iPhone.
Tap the box, choose Photo Library, and pick your HEIC photos.
Tap each PNG and use the Share icon to save it to Photos or Files.

faq.q: Why pick PNG instead of JPG on my iPhone?
faq.a: PNG keeps every pixel with no compression loss, which is better for editing or marking up a photo. The trade-off is a larger file than JPG.

modifiers.label: on Windows
modifiers.intro: Windows can't open HEIC unless you buy the HEVC codec from the Microsoft Store, and even then editing is awkward. Converting HEIC to lossless PNG lets you open and edit the photo in Paint, Photoshop, or any Windows app with full quality. This runs in your browser on any Windows PC — no codec purchase, no software install, no upload.
howto: Open this page in Edge, Chrome, or Firefox on Windows.
Drag your HEIC files into the box.
Download the lossless PNG files.

faq.q: Do I need the paid HEVC extension?
faq.a: No. The HEIC is decoded in your browser, so you don't need any Windows codec or Store purchase.

tools.title: WebP to PNG
tools.metaDesc: Convert WebP to PNG free in your browser. No upload, no watermark, keeps transparency.
tools.intro: You downloaded an image and it came as a WebP file that your editor or an old app refuses to open. This converter turns WebP into a universally supported PNG while preserving transparency. It runs in your browser — the image is never uploaded anywhere.
howto: Drag your WebP images into the box above.
Each image is decoded by your browser and saved as PNG.
Download the PNG files.

faq.q: Does this keep transparency?
faq.a: Yes. PNG supports an alpha channel, so transparent areas in the WebP are preserved.

faq.q: Why was my image a WebP?
faq.a: Many sites serve WebP because it is smaller. Saving it locally keeps the WebP format, which some programs can't open.

modifiers.label: with transparent background
modifiers.intro: The main reason to convert WebP to PNG instead of JPG is transparency. Logos, stickers, and product cut-outs often have see-through areas that JPG would replace with an ugly white box. This page preserves the full alpha channel, so transparent stays transparent in the PNG. As always, the image is converted in your browser and never uploaded.
howto: Drag your WebP image into the box above.
The alpha channel is preserved exactly — nothing is flattened.
Download the PNG, then drop it onto a colored background to confirm transparency survived.

faq.q: How do I check the transparency was kept?
faq.a: Open the PNG over a colored background, for example in a slide or editor. Transparent areas should show the background through them.

faq.q: What if my WebP has no transparency?
faq.a: Then you simply get a normal opaque PNG — nothing breaks.

tools.title: WebP to JPG
tools.metaDesc: Convert WebP to JPG free in your browser. No upload, no watermark.
tools.intro: Need a plain JPG from a WebP image? This converter re-encodes WebP as JPG in your browser, flattening any transparency onto a white background so the result opens everywhere. Nothing is uploaded — the whole process is local.
howto: Drag your WebP images into the box above.
Transparent areas are flattened to white and saved as JPG.
Download the JPG files.

faq.q: What happens to transparency?
faq.a: JPG has no transparency, so transparent pixels become white. If you need transparency, convert to PNG instead.

tools.title: PNG to JPG
tools.metaDesc: Convert PNG to JPG free in your browser. Smaller files, no upload, no watermark.
tools.intro: PNG files can be large, especially for photos. Converting PNG to JPG shrinks the file size dramatically while keeping the image looking great — ideal for email, web pages, and sharing. Conversion happens in your browser, so your images stay private.
howto: Drag your PNG images into the box above.
Each is re-encoded as a compact JPG (transparency flattened to white).
Download the JPG files.

faq.q: Why convert PNG to JPG?
faq.a: JPG files are much smaller for photographs, which makes them faster to send and upload.

modifiers.label: without losing quality
modifiers.intro: This page encodes JPG at high quality (92%) and keeps the original resolution, so the visual difference from your PNG is negligible while the file gets much smaller. For photos this is the sweet spot between size and quality.
howto: Drop your PNG into the box.
We keep full resolution and use a high quality setting.
Download and compare — it should look identical at normal sizes.

faq.q: Can JPG match PNG exactly?
faq.a: Not pixel-for-pixel — JPG is lossy. But at 92% quality the difference is invisible to the eye for most images.

tools.title: JPG to PNG
tools.metaDesc: Convert JPG to PNG free in your browser. Lossless PNG output, no upload, no watermark.
tools.intro: Converting JPG to PNG gives you a lossless copy that won't degrade further when you edit and re-save it, and PNG is the right choice when you need a format that supports transparency. The conversion is done in your browser — your images are never uploaded.
howto: Drag your JPG images into the box above.
Each is re-encoded as a lossless PNG.
Download the PNG files.

faq.q: Does converting to PNG add transparency?
faq.a: No — a JPG has no transparency to begin with. PNG simply gives you a lossless container and the option to add transparency later in an editor.

tools.title: SVG to PNG
tools.metaDesc: Convert SVG to PNG free in your browser. Crisp rendering, no upload, no watermark.
tools.intro: SVG is a vector format, perfect for logos and icons, but many tools and platforms need a raster PNG instead. Your browser is actually the most accurate SVG renderer there is, so converting in-browser gives a crisp, correct PNG — with nothing uploaded to a server.
howto: Drag your SVG files into the box above.
Your browser renders each SVG and exports it as PNG.
Download the PNG files.

faq.q: What resolution do I get?
faq.a: By default the PNG matches the SVG's intrinsic size. See the high-resolution page to export at 2x or 4x.

modifiers.label: in high resolution
modifiers.intro: An SVG is infinitely scalable, but the PNG you export from it is not — so the export resolution matters. This page renders your SVG at 4x its native size, producing a crisp, high-resolution PNG that stays sharp on retina screens, in print, as an app icon, or as a large thumbnail. The rendering is done by your own browser, locally.
howto: Drag your SVG files into the box above.
Each SVG is rendered at 4x its intrinsic size.
Download the high-resolution PNG.

faq.q: How large will the PNG be?
faq.a: Four times the width and height of the SVG's intrinsic size, so a 256px icon becomes a 1024px PNG.

faq.q: Why not just open the SVG larger?
faq.a: Many platforms only accept raster images. Rendering at 4x keeps the result sharp where a 1x export would look soft when enlarged.

modifiers.label: with white background
modifiers.intro: SVGs are usually transparent, so a normal SVG to PNG export has a see-through background — which looks wrong on a white document or breaks if you later save it as JPG. This page renders your SVG onto a solid white background, giving you a PNG with no transparency that drops cleanly into documents, slides, and print. Everything happens in your browser.
howto: Drag your SVG files into the box above.
Each SVG is rendered on a solid white background.
Download the PNG — no transparency, ready for documents.

faq.q: Can I get a transparent PNG instead?
faq.a: Yes — use the standard SVG to PNG page, which keeps the transparent background.

faq.q: Why white specifically?
faq.a: White matches most documents, slides, and printed pages, so the image blends in without an odd colored box.

tools.title: AVIF to JPG
tools.metaDesc: Convert AVIF to JPG free in your browser. No upload, no watermark.
tools.intro: AVIF is a modern, highly efficient image format, but plenty of apps still can't open it. This converter turns AVIF into a universally compatible JPG using your browser's built-in AVIF decoder — locally, with no upload.
howto: Drag your AVIF images into the box above.
Each is decoded by your browser and saved as JPG.
Download the JPG files.

faq.q: Why can't I open AVIF files?
faq.a: AVIF is new, so older software hasn't added support yet. Converting to JPG fixes compatibility everywhere.

modifiers.label: on Android
modifiers.intro: Chrome on Android often saves images as AVIF, and then your gallery, a messenger, or a photo editor refuses to open them. This page converts AVIF to a universally compatible JPG right inside your Android browser, so the image works everywhere you want to use it — with nothing uploaded to a server.
howto: Open this page in Chrome on your Android phone.
Tap the box and choose the AVIF file from Files or Downloads.
Download the JPG — it saves to your Downloads folder.

faq.q: Why did my download arrive as AVIF?
faq.a: Many sites serve AVIF to Chrome because it is smaller and faster. Saving it keeps that format, which older apps can't open.

faq.q: Do I need to install an app?
faq.a: No. The conversion runs in your Android browser.
